title: Acacia pseudoglaucophylla
taxonomy: Acacia pseudoglaucophylla is classified as a species of shrub or small tree in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. This genus, Acacia, is a large and diverse group of plants that are native to Africa, Australia, and Asia. Within the Acacia genus, Acacia pseudoglaucophylla is a member of the subgenus Phyllodineae, which is characterized by leaves that are modified into phyllodes. The species Acacia pseudoglaucophylla is endemic to Australia and has been reported to occur in the Northern Territory and Western Australia. Further classification may include the species being assigned to a specific series within the Acacia genus.
